title: Spermacoce oldenlandiae
taxonomy: Spermacoce oldenlandiae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the genus Spermacoce, which comprises a large number of species. Spermacoce oldenlandiae is a native plant found in various regions, including Australia and Asia. The classification of this plant places it within the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. This classification system is based on the botanical nomenclature rules established by the International Code of Nomenclature for algae, fungi, and plants.

intro: Spermacoce oldenlandiae, also known as Thunbergia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Spermacoce oldenlandiae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Spermacoce oldenlandiae is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of a paste or powder, often mixed with other herbs to treat various ailments.
